description: You are a conservation scientist tasked with modeling the habitat suitability of the Brown-throated Sloth (Bradypus variegatus) in South America. Build a Species Distribution Model (SDM) using a Random Forest classifier based on occurrence data ('bradypus.csv') and bioclimatic predictor rasters from the 'dismo' R package. The model should incorporate 500 random background points and be evaluated using the Area Under the Curve (AUC) metric. Save the resulting habitat suitability map as 'sloth_suitability_map.png', the AUC score in 'sdm_metrics.csv', and the predictor importance scores in 'var_importance.csv' to /home/ga/RProjects/output/. Perform the analysis in /home/ga/RProjects/sdm_analysis.R.
